location.office_time_required: Monday, Wednesday, and Fridays in office

level_signals: 5 to 8 years of product management experience
Significant ownership of ML powered products or platform systems
Owns product strategy and roadmap for a core area

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5 to 8 years of product management experience with significant ownership of ML powered products or platform systems.
Deep understanding of how to measure and improve model quality, including evaluation frameworks, annotation pipelines, and benchmark design.
Strong technical fluency across ML, data pipelines, and distributed systems.
Experience working closely with ML researchers and engineers to drive impact in production.
Ability to balance long term architectural investments with near term quality improvements.
Strong communication skills and the ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear decisions and narratives.
A track record of delivering high quality products in domains where accuracy, reliability, and trust are paramount.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have experience building evaluation platforms, ML observability systems, or quality measurement pipelines.
You have worked in clinical, healthcare, or regulated environments with a high bar for accuracy and compliance.
You have worked on specialty specific or domain specific model adaptations.
You have worked on personalization systems, context ingestion frameworks, or ambient intelligence products.
You have experience shipping large scale ML products with human in the loop workflows.

scope.surface: Note Generation within the Note Quality group — note models, evaluation systems, specialty and workflow engines
core_responsibilities: Drive AI product strategy and execution within Note Generation
Shape the roadmap for a core area of Abridge's note models and evaluation stack
Help build a world class note quality evaluation system across human annotation, LLM judges, automated backtesting, and in production analytics
Own specialty priorities and ensure specialty notes reflect clinical expectations
Partner with clinical leaders to define gold standards
Work closely with engineering and ML to align on architecture, model iteration schedules, evaluation methodologies, and system reliability
Partner with product design to define user control surfaces
Translate real world feedback into the model development loop

compensation.equity_form: equity grants for full time employees

green_flags: Clear product ownership area (Note Generation / Note Quality group)
Mission-driven company with a concrete product (clinical documentation AI)
Strong cross-functional collaboration scope including clinical leaders and specialty councils
Equity grants mentioned as part of compensation
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
Explicit encouragement to apply if willing to relocate — signals openness to candidates outside SF
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed — no base salary stated
Hard hybrid requirement (Mon/Wed/Fri in San Francisco office) eliminates remote candidates
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in JD
No team size or headcount context provided
Title says 'Product Lead' but body consistently refers to the role as 'Product Manager' — possible scope ambiguity
